Name: Users can create security groups
Description:
This indicator checks if non-admin users can create security groups.

Required permissions

  • Policy.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
By default all users are allowed to create security groups in an Entra tenant.

While many organizations may desire users to be able to create M365 groups for collaborations, valid cases for users creating security groups are generally limited.

Entra ID does not require unique group names, and an attacker may create malicious security groups with duplicate names of valid groups, in an effort to have the malicious group assigned further privileges.

References

Default user permissions - Microsoft Entra | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: Any user can create security groups
Remediation:
Disable Users can create security groups in the Entra tenant.

To disable, open the Microsoft Entra admin center (direct link), expand Identity, expand Users, and select User settings.

In the blade on the right, toggle Users can create security groups to no.

Select Save at the bottom of the blade.

Organizations should understand how this change impacts their users and communicate the change out according to defined business processes.
